---
title: "HALT"
format: html
---

# HALT: Hungry, Angry, Lonely, Tired

HALT is a quick risk check used when trying to stay alcohol-free. Hunger, anger, loneliness, and tiredness each lower coping capacity and increase impulsive risk. When two or more HALT states are active, risk often rises quickly.

HALT works as a preventive scan for high-risk states. Catching these states early allows quick corrective action before urges escalate or clear thinking gets harder.

**HALT is MOST useful when used preventively, not only during crisis.**

![](https://github.com/jjcurtin/aud_support/blob/main/modules/images/HALT.png?raw=true){fig-align="center"}

## How to Use This Tool

-   Use in under 2 minutes\
-   Check at predictable times (before events, after work, evenings)\
-   Act **before urges escalate**

## Take a moment to check-in

| State  | Level (0–10) | Body Signs | Immediate Action |
|--------|-------------:|------------|------------------|
| Hungry |              |            |                  |
| Angry  |              |            |                  |
| Lonely |              |            |                  |
| Tired  |              |            |                  |

::: callout
## Examples of Immediate Action

-   **Hungry** → Eat a snack with protein/carbs\
-   **Angry** → Pause, breathe, step away, write thoughts\
-   **Lonely** → Text/call someone, sit near others\
-   **Tired** → Rest, hydrate, reduce stimulation\
:::

## Quick re-check

After taking action:

-   New levels (0–10):
    -   Hungry: \_\_\_\_\_
    -   Angry: \_\_\_\_\_
    -   Lonely: \_\_\_\_\_
    -   Tired: \_\_\_\_\_
-   What changed?

**Remember: You don’t need to feel “bad enough” to act. Early action prevents bigger problems.**
